questions.question: 中文导读：What is an eigenvector of a matrix?
options: 中文选项：The largest row in the matrix
中文选项：A vector that the matrix only scales (never rotates) when multiplied
中文选项：A vector perpendicular to all columns of the matrix
中文选项：The diagonal entries of the matrix expressed as a vector
questions.explanation: 中文解析：An eigenvector v satisfies Av = lambda*v, meaning the matrix A only stretches v by the scalar factor lambda (the eigenvalue) without changing its direction.

questions.question: 中文导读：What does the determinant of a 2D transformation matrix represent geometrically?
options: 中文选项：The angle of rotation applied by the matrix
中文选项：The factor by which the matrix scales area
中文选项：The number of eigenvectors the matrix has
中文选项：The trace of the matrix
questions.explanation: 中文解析：The determinant measures how much the transformation scales area. det=1 preserves area (rotation), det=2 doubles area, det=0 crushes to a lower dimension, and det=-1 preserves area but flips orientation.

questions.question: 中文导读：Why does the order of matrix transformations matter? (i.e., why is R @ S different from S @ R?)
options: 中文选项：Matrix addition is not commutative
中文选项：Matrix multiplication is not commutative: rotating then scaling gives a different result than scaling then rotating
中文选项：The determinants are different for each order
中文选项：One order produces a larger matrix than the other
questions.explanation: 中文解析：Matrix multiplication is not commutative. Rotating (1,0) by 90 degrees then scaling by (2,0.5) gives (0,0.5), but scaling first then rotating gives (0,2). The geometric operations compose differently.

questions.question: 中文导读：In a recurrent neural network, what happens when the weight matrix has eigenvalues with magnitude greater than 1?
options: 中文选项：The network learns faster
中文选项：Outputs explode exponentially over time steps (exploding gradient problem)
中文选项：The network becomes more stable
中文选项：The eigenvalues converge to 1 over training
questions.explanation: 中文解析：Repeated multiplication by a matrix amplifies the eigenvalue directions. Eigenvalues > 1 cause exponential growth (exploding gradients), while eigenvalues < 1 cause exponential decay (vanishing gradients).

questions.question: 中文导读：The matrix A = [[2, 1], [1, 2]] has eigenvalues 3 and 1. What does eigendecomposition A = V @ D @ V^(-1) reveal?
options: 中文选项：A is equivalent to two rotations
中文选项：A stretches space by 3x along the [1,1] direction and leaves the [1,-1] direction unchanged
中文选项：A compresses all vectors by a factor of 2
中文选项：A has rank 1 and maps all vectors to a line
questions.explanation: 中文解析：The eigenvalue 3 with eigenvector [1,1] means A stretches 3x along the diagonal. The eigenvalue 1 with eigenvector [1,-1] means A leaves the anti-diagonal unchanged. D holds {3,1}, V holds the eigenvectors.
